fresh_facts.claim: The U.S. military formally began a blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas on April 13, 2026, at 10 a.m. EDT following the collapse of U.S.-Iran peace talks.
President Trump announced that U.S. forces would intercept any ship on global seas that continued to pay transit tolls or fees to Iran.
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) threatened that one wrong move by the United States would plunge U.S. forces into a 'deadly whirlpool of destruction.'
Iran asserts 'full strategic authority' over the Strait of Hormuz, with Iranian officials warning that any hostile action could lead to further escalation.
Trump and his advisers are studying plans for limited military strikes on Iran while also weighing the blockade option as combined pressure tactics.
U.S. intelligence indicates that China is expected to deliver advanced air defense systems to Iran within the next few weeks.
China rejected U.S. intelligence claims that it is preparing to supply weapons to Iran, calling the allegations 'baseless smears.'
U.S. Vice President JD Vance is set to lead a delegation to Pakistan with envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to negotiate terms with Iran.
President Trump vows to keep U.S. troops in the Persian Gulf until a 'real agreement' is fully complied with.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces fierce hardline backlash for signaling conditional war-end willingness.
The IRGC forced President Pezeshkian's appointment of hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council secretary.
The IRGC has assumed control over key state functions amid political deadlock in Iran.
Succession dynamics center on Mojtaba Khamenei's rise as the logical outcome of Ali Khamenei's engineered system.
Iran's economy shows structural breakdown beyond high inflation, with a shift away from faith in its currency following the war.
Authorities have intensified crackdowns with over 700 arrests by late June, including mass detentions and warrantless raids.
Fatal IRGC/Basij shootings occurred in Hamedan and Khomein on July 2, targeting dissident regions.
Iran Human Rights Organization reports that state repression is ramping up via executions and vague espionage laws.
Amnesty International's Karg notes tightened domestic control and rising executions amid international isolation.
Former president Rouhani called the ceasefire a chance for economic resilience and trust-building on July 2.
CSIS analysis highlights unresolved nuclear ambitions and rising terrorism risks under the ceasefire.

regime_fall.current_status: The U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports (begun April 13) has intensified military pressure while the ceasefire deteriorates, with Iran's economy showing structural breakdown and authorities conducting mass arrests and lethal crackdowns on dissidents by early July, signaling regime consolidation through force amid succession uncertainty and diplomatic stalling.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Fatal IRGC/Basij shootings occurred in Hamedan and Khomein on July 2, with authorities conducting over 700 arrests by late June as state repression escalates via executions targeting dissident regions.
